title: MADRaT based package on factor inputs
text: package provides functions MAgPIE input data factor inputs agricultural production (focus capital labor).

title: MADRaT based package on factor inputs
text: installation recent package version additional repository added R: additional repository can made available permanently adding line file called .Rprofile stored home folder system (Sys.glob("~") R returns home directory). recent version package can installed using install.packages: Package updates can installed using update.packages (make sure additional repository added running command):

title: calcHourlyLaborCosts — calcHourlyLaborCosts
text: calculates dataset hourly labor costs per employee agriculture

title: calcHourlyLaborCosts — calcHourlyLaborCosts
code: calcHourlyLaborCosts(   datasource = "USDA_FAO",   dataVersionILO = "Aug24",   sector = "agriculture",   fillWithRegression = TRUE,   calibYear = 2010,   cutAfterCalibYear = TRUE,   projection = FALSE )

title: calcHourlyLaborCosts — calcHourlyLaborCosts
text: datasource either raw data "ILO" (agriculture+forestry+fishery) data calculated based total labor costs "USDA_FAO" (crop+livestock production). dataVersionILO version ILO data use (hourly labor costs source ILO, ag empl. source USDA_FAO). "" oldest version, "monthYear" (e.g. "Aug24") newer version) sector average hourly labor costs reported ("agriculture"), hourly labor costs specific either "crops" "livestock" production. ILO aggregate hourly labor costs available. fillWithRegression boolean: missing values filled based regression ILO hourly labor costs GDPpcMER (calibrated countries) calibYear case fillWithRegression TRUE, data year ignored calculated using regression (calibrated year calibYear, recent year data calibYear). NULL data used calibration cutAfterCalibYear boolean, relevant fillWithRegression TRUE. cutAfterCalibYear TRUE, raw data calib year overwritten regression results (necessary consistency calculation within MAgPIE). FALSE, raw data kept gaps filled regression projection either FALSE SSP projections based. relevant fillWithRegression TRUE.

title: calcLaborCosts — calcLaborCosts
text: datasource data source labor costs based. Available ILO, USDA (also uses data VoP FAO), GTAP. dataVersionILO source ILO, version can chosen. "" oldest version, "monthYear" (e.g. "Aug24") newer version) subsectors boolean: output aggregated split available subsectors (crops, livst, forestry, fishery) inclFish boolean: fish labor costs included? inclForest boolean: forestry labor costs included (available ILO GTAP)? otherLivst boolean: other_livst category (.e. beeswax, wool, silkworms, honey) included livestock (relevant datasource USDA)? gtapVar variable name use GTAP (relevant source "GTAP") addSubsidies boolean: subsidy data (IFPRI) added VoP applying USDA cost shares (relevant datasource "USDA")

text: function calculates regression parameters (b) function Share=*log10(GDP)+b share adjusted share capital labour.

text: datasource USDA available caseStudies case studies used regression (either CountryCaseStudies CaseStudiesDirectMapping). Default CountryCaseStudies, including regional case studies weakens direct link GDP per capita (results regression non-normally distributed residuals).

title: regressionsILO — calcRegressionsILO
text: Calculates regression coefficients used calc functions ILO data sets

title: regressionsILO — calcRegressionsILO
code: calcRegressionsILO(   subtype = "AgEmplShare",   dataVersionILO = "Aug24",   thresholdWage = 0.1,   forceWageIntercept = TRUE,   wageRegrType = NULL,   recalculate = FALSE )

title: regressionsILO — calcRegressionsILO
text: subtype specifies regression type: "AgEmplShare" regression square root share people employed agriculture (total population) log (base 10) GDP pc PPP05. "HourlyLaborCosts" regression  mean nominal hourly labor cost per employee agriculture GDP pc MER05. dataVersionILO version ILO input data regression use. "" oldest version old regression, "monthYear" (e.g. "Aug24") newer data new regression type thresholdWage relevant linear hourly labor cost regression: low GDP pc MER, regression hourly labor costs GDP pc MER can lead unreasonably low even negative hourly labor costs. Therefore, set hourly labor costs threshold threshold. forceWageIntercept relevant linear hourly labor cost regression: TRUE, wage threshold also used intercept regression. FALSE, intercept determined regression wageRegrType relevant HourlyLaborCosts regression. NULL, linear regression used oldest data (dataVersionILO: ""), loglog regression newer data. Can overwritten specifically setting wageRegrType "linear" "loglog". recalculate whether regression read source folder, recalculated scratch. Recalculation can lead new regression coefficients data changed, result always checked.

title: regressionsILO — calcRegressionsILO
text: Debbora Leip

title: readILOSTAT — readILOSTAT
text: subtype Type ILOSTAT data downloaded, version specified suffic "_MonthYear" (month year download) `EmplByActivityModelled`: "Employment sex economic activity – ILO modelled estimates, Nov. 2020 (thousands)" `WeeklyHoursByActivity`: "Mean weekly hours actually worked per employed person sex economic activity" `HourlyLaborCostsByActivity`: "Mean nominal hourly labour cost per employee economic activity" `EmplByISIC2`: "Employment sex economic activity - ISIC level 2 (thousands) | Annual" `EmplByActivityMonthly`: "Employment sex economic activity (thousands) | Monthly" `EmplByActivityMonthlyAdj`: "Employment sex economic activity, seasonally adjusted series (thousands) | Monthly" `EmplByActivityAndStatus`: "Employment sex, status employment economic activity (thousands) | Annual" `WeeklyHoursByActivityMonthly`: "Mean weekly hours actually worked per employee sex economic  activity | Monthly" `WeeklyHoursByISIC2`: "Mean weekly hours actually worked per employed person sex economic  activity - ISIC level 2 | Annual" `WeeklyHoursEmployeesByISIC2`: "Mean weekly hours actually worked per employee sex economic  activity - ISIC level 2 | Annual" `LaborIncomeShareGDPModelled`: "Labour income share percent GDP – ILO modelled estimates,  July 2019 ( `OutputPerWorkerModelled`: "Output per worker (GDP constant 2010 US $) – ILO modelled estimates,  Nov. 2020 | Annual"

text: Debbora Leip
